tool.name: zap_action
tool.description:
Run OWASP ZAP security scans against in-scope web application targets. ZAP runs as a dedicated Docker container (zaproxy/zap-stable) alongside the sandbox.
Typical workflow: 1. zap_action spider target=https://app.com — crawl the app 2. zap_action status — wait until spider progress=100 3. zap_action ajax_spider target=https://app.com — crawl JS-rendered pages (SPAs) 4. zap_action status — wait until ajax_spider_running=false 5. zap_action active_scan target=https://app.com — run active vulnerability tests 6. zap_action status — wait until active scan progress=100 7. zap_action alerts — retrieve all findings
Load the zap skill before first use: load_skill skill_name=zap Only use this tool on in-scope targets. ZAP active scanning is intrusive.

parameters.parameter: Action to perform. One of: spider — Traditional spider: crawl links and forms. ajax_spider — AJAX spider: crawl JS-heavy SPAs with headless browser. active_scan — Active vulnerability scanner (intrusive, run after spider). status — Get progress of all running spiders and active scans. alerts — Retrieve vulnerability findings (run after scans complete). stop — Stop a running spider or active scan.
Target URL for spider, ajax_spider, or active_scan actions. Must be an in-scope authorized target (e.g., https://app.example.com).
ZAP scan ID returned by spider or active_scan actions. Used with alerts (filter by scan) and stop (stop specific scan).
Maximum number of alerts to return from the alerts action. Default: 100.
